I will want his hearing just a few days to monitor the reduction in her insulin dose that I will empirically try.

other_active_problems.name: DIABETES MELLITUS TYPE II
other_active_problems.assessment:
DIABETES MELLITUS TYPE II (Working Diagnosis), Fair Control.


Note:

Had allergic reaction when taking Byetta.  generalized bad feeling along with feeling of dread.  No evidence of anaphylaxis.  Stopped taking byetta after that incident.  Back on just insulin.  Sugars in past few days have been the same as before: readings good before meals but then very poor in between meals.



Will try going to Lantus now and Humalog before meals rather than try out a pill type regimen. Glucophage may be useful in future.



Currently taking Novolin 33 and 22 which is about 55 units long acting. I suspect that is a bit of overinsulination and not just resistance. Pretty common with NPH. Usual dose is 0.6 u per Kg, in her case, she should be closer to 42 units a day total. 



Will start Lantus at 35 units and Humalog before each meal and see how she does. Will start tonite.

Special Instructions:



Instructions for Sliding Scale Fast Acting Insulin.  This is your Sliding Scale for your fast acting insulin. The amount of insulin is  based on your blood sugar result before meals:



Breakfast



61-90  7 units

91-130 13 u

201-250   18 u

351 and over 20 u



Lunch
